description: This micrograph presents the initial fully recrystallized equiaxed ferritic microstructure of Fe-8wt%Al low-density ferritic steel, with a 500 μm scale bar for dimensional reference. The sample is fabricated via three-pass hot rolling with a cumulative true strain of 1, yielding 100% recrystallized, isotropic single-phase A2-structured ferrite grains with relatively uniform size distribution. This microstructure serves as the uniform baseline for subsequent hot torsion tests performed under 900-1100℃ and strain rates of 0.1-10 s⁻¹, which simulate hot rolling conditions to explore continuous and discontinuous dynamic recrystallization mechanisms. The figure supports that the consistent initial recrystallized microstructure eliminates pre-existing texture interference, ensuring reliable identification of dynamic recrystallization process windows to optimize rolling processes for reducing deep-drawing roping defects of the alloy.
